calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-31-28 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted; Declaration Of David Bartley, Esq.; Notice Of Lodging Of Proposed Order;
Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admissions As Admitted is GRANTED.

Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(c), upon motion the court shall deem unanswered requests for admissions (RFAs) admitted, unless it finds the responding party, before hearing on the motion, served a proposed response to the requests for admission that is in substantial compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.220.

The record here demonstrates Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. sent requests for admissions that appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Defendant Vernell W Lawson ultimately failed to answer/respond. The record does not demonstrate his failure was reasonable or justified. The requests set out in Plaintiff's Request for Admissions, Set One, executed June 18, 2025, are deemed admitted and the genuineness of the relevant documents is established.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
